name: Air (Live Reload for Go Applications)

performance.notes: File watcher + rebuild time; typical Go app ~500ms to recompile

agent_readiness.notes: Live reload for Go development. air in project root (or air init for .air.toml). Watches .go files, rebuilds on save, restarts server. .air.toml config: root, cmd ('go build -o ./tmp/main .'), include_ext, exclude_dir. Colored build output. Custom build commands. Used by essentially all Go web development workflows. The nodemon for Go. Simple and reliable.

gotchas: Build errors shown in terminal (no browser overlay like webpack HMR)
Large projects have longer rebuild times
Windows file watching sometimes less reliable than Linux/Mac
best_when: Any Go web development requiring live reload
Go HTTP servers, APIs, or full-stack apps
avoid_when: Projects where go run ./... is fast enough
summary: Air provides live reload for Go applications. Watches .go files, rebuilds on save, restarts the server. air init creates .air.toml config. The nodemon equivalent for Go. Used by essentially all Go web developers. Simple and reliable. Development only — use compiled binaries in production.
